title: States Weekly: June 17, 2026
gist: A roundup of June 2026 regulatory and policy moves across Indian states, with notable activity in battery energy storage and grid frameworks, AI adoption in healthcare, investment-approval streamlining, and welfare/austerity measures. The clearest investor signals are new BESS, grid-code and open-access regulations plus deemed-clearance investment timelines.

insights.claim: Tamil Nadu issued a Draft Notification of TNERC (Battery Energy Storage Systems) Regulations, 2026, providing a comprehensive framework for battery energy storage systems in the state.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: A dedicated BESS regulatory framework in a major state derisks storage project economics; watch Indian storage developers and integrators.
insights.locator: Tamil Nadu issues Draft Notification of TNERC

insights.claim: Karnataka released Draft KERC Connectivity and General Network Access regulations for 2026 to create a more structured and transparent framework for open access and network connectivity across the state.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Clearer open-access rules ease C&I renewable procurement, supporting third-party power developers in Karnataka.

insights.claim: Meghalaya issued Draft MSERC State Grid Code Regulations, 2026, setting guidelines for planning, operation, scheduling and protection of the state power grid.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: New state grid codes standardize integration rules, relevant for renewable interconnection in smaller states.
insights.locator: Meghalaya issues Draft Meghalaya State Electricity

insights.claim: Delhi temporarily relaxed Regulation 172 of DERC tariff rules to allow DISCOMs to recover a higher Fuel and Power Purchase Adjustment Surcharge from consumers.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Higher FPPAS recovery improves DISCOM cash flow and signals rising power costs being passed to consumers.

insights.claim: Punjab approved an additional surcharge of INR 1.13 per kWh for full open access consumers under PSPCL, ordered by the PSERC.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Open-access surcharges raise the cost of third-party power procurement in Punjab, a headwind for C&I open-access economics.

insights.claim: West Bengal mandated that all state government offices move to prepaid smart electricity meters and clear outstanding power dues in a lump sum or over 300 days.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Government smart-metering mandates expand the addressable market for smart-meter and metering-tech vendors.
insights.locator: West Bengal mandates that all state government offices

insights.claim: Bihar mandated that all investment proposals receive a decision within 30 days, with automatic deemed clearance granted if authorities fail to decide within the limit.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Deemed-clearance timelines materially de-risk project approval in Bihar, an ease-of-doing-business signal for inbound investment.
insights.locator: Bihar mandates approvals for all investment proposals

insights.claim: Andhra Pradesh launched a pilot using AI systems to analyze patient eye images and determine the severity of diabetic retinopathy.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Public-sector AI diagnostics adoption in Indian states signals a growing healthtech-AI procurement market.
insights.locator: Andhra Pradesh launches a pilot initiative

insights.claim: Uttarakhand announced the Uttarakhand Science, Technology and Innovation Policy 2026, providing a platform for youth, researchers, startups and innovators and encouraging the merging of traditional knowledge with modern technology.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: State innovation policies expand startup-support frameworks but rarely move markets near-term.

insights.claim: Maharashtra decentralized approval of government land deals, empowering District Collectors and Divisional Commissioners to approve sale, transfer, redevelopment and change of use within certain land-value limits.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Faster local land-deal approvals can accelerate real-estate and industrial-land transactions in Maharashtra.

insights.claim: Uttar Pradesh launched Ganga, a scheme to provide high-speed fiber-based broadband connectivity to thousands of rural households across the state.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Rural broadband buildout supports telecom and fiber infrastructure demand in India's largest state.

insights.claim: Haryana issued austerity and resource-management guidelines effective immediately through September 2026, including restrictions on foreign travel, more virtual meetings, fuel-consumption reduction targets and energy-saving measures in government buildings.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: State-level austerity signals fiscal tightening that can dampen government procurement and discretionary spend.
insights.locator: Haryana issues a set of austerity

insights.claim: Kerala approved free bus travel for women and transgender individuals on KSRTC ordinary services from June 15, 2026, and Karnataka announced free bus passes for students.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Expanding transit-subsidy schemes add to state welfare spending pressures relevant to fiscal-balance watchers.
insights.locator: Kerala approves a free bus travel scheme

insights.claim: Odisha announced free education in government schools and colleges from kindergarten to postgraduate level to make education universally accessible.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Universal free-education commitments increase state social spending obligations.
